title: Spatial memory and non-invasive closed-loop stimulus timing
description: This dataset contains behavioral and electrophysiological recordings from a hybrid spatial-navigation and free recall experiment designed to investigate memory encoding dynamics and test closed-loop stimulus timing optimization. Participants performed a virtual courier task involving spatial navigation and item delivery, followed by recall testing, across read-only baseline sessions and classifier-guided closed-loop sessions where stimulus presentation was timed to coincide with predicted memory encoding states. The dataset provides a resource for studying neural correlates of spatial and episodic memory in naturalistic task contexts. Note: Memory dynamics in closed-loop sessions may be influenced by the closed-loop timing manipulation and should be interpreted accordingly. Read-only sessions were not subject to this manipulation and are suitable for analyses of general mnemonic function.
methods_description: The experiment employed a hybrid spatial-navigation and free recall paradigm conducted at the University of Pennsylvania's Computational Memory Lab (2021-2022). Participants completed read-only sessions to generate training data for memory classifiers, followed by closed-loop sessions where classifier-based predictions guided stimulus presentation timing. Intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral event logs were collected throughout both session types. Memory dynamics in closed-loop sessions may reflect the influence of the closed-loop timing manipulation.

funding_references.funder_name: U.S. Army Medical Research and Development Command
funding_references.award_title: Restoring memory with task-independent semi-chronic closed-loop direct brain stimulation and non-invasive closed-loop stimulus timing optimization
